items.text: Hi Marcus, Our records show your meal plan ended with an unused balance of $42.50. A refund will be issued to your student account by Friday, May 22. If you re-enroll before then, the credit instead transfers to next term. Best, Dining Services

questions.stem: According to the email, where will the unused balance be sent?
questions.explanation: The email states a refund will be issued to Marcus's student account by Friday, May 22.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who re-enroll before May 22?

options.A: They forfeit the leftover money entirely
options.B: They must contact Dining Services directly
options.C: They keep the money for future dining
options.D: They receive an additional bonus credit

questions.explanation: Because re-enrolling transfers the credit to next term, those students keep the money for future dining rather than receiving a refund.

items.text: Campus Wi-Fi maintenance is scheduled for Tuesday, June 9, from 1:00 AM to 4:00 AM. During this three-hour window, wireless access in all residence halls will be unavailable. Wired connections in the library will remain active. Service is expected to resume automatically afterward.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how long will the maintenance last?
questions.explanation: The window runs from 1:00 AM to 4:00 AM, a span of three hours, which the notice calls a three-hour window.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will still function during the window?
questions.explanation: The notice says wired connections in the library will remain active during the maintenance.

items.text: FREE FLU SHOTS — No Appointment Needed! Protect yourself this season. Thursday, October 16, 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM at the Student Health Center, Room 110. Bring your student ID. Sleeves up, in and out in minutes. Walk-ins welcome all afternoon. Stay healthy, Wildcats!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what should attendees bring?
questions.explanation: The flyer instructs attendees to bring their student ID.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about scheduling for the clinic?
questions.explanation: Because the flyer says no appointment is needed and walk-ins are welcome, advance booking is unnecessary.

items.text: Effective Monday, April 6, the recycling bins outside Foster Hall will be relocated to the courtyard near Gate 3. A $5 fee will be charged for any bin reserved for private events. Questions should be directed to the Facilities Office before the moving date.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where will the bins be moved?
questions.explanation: The notice states the bins will be relocated to the courtyard near Gate 3.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about reserving a bin for a private event?

options.A: It is prohibited entirely on campus

questions.explanation: Because a $5 fee applies to any bin reserved for private events, reserving one involves an extra charge.
